# sesam tell guesses --user from a forge recipient spec (github:/gitlab:/codeberg:)
# when --user is omitted, and errors clearly when it cannot guess.

exec git init
exec git config user.email test@sesam.dev
exec git config user.name 'Sesam Test'

exec sesam init --user admin
exec git add -A
exec git commit -m 'init sesam'

# Negative: a raw (non-forge) recipient with no --user cannot be guessed.
# The guess fails before any key resolution, so this is fully offline.
! exec sesam tell --recipient $BOB_PUBKEY --group dev
stderr 'failed to guess user name'
! stdout 'guessed'

# Positive: a github: spec yields the handle after the colon. The guess is
# printed (stdout) before key resolution; resolving the forge user then fails
# without network access, so we assert only the guessed name - not the tell
# outcome - and that the guess itself did not error. The '.' wildcards match
# the literal quotes around --user.
! exec sesam tell --recipient github:octobot-sesam-test-xyzzy --group dev
stdout 'guessed .--user octobot-sesam-test-xyzzy. from github:octobot-sesam-test-xyzzy'
! stderr 'failed to guess user name'

# Positive: the first forge spec wins even when mixed with a raw recipient,
# and the guess is generic across supported forges (gitlab here).
! exec sesam tell --recipient $BOB_PUBKEY --recipient gitlab:glhandle-sesam-test-xyzzy --group dev
stdout 'guessed .--user glhandle-sesam-test-xyzzy. from gitlab:glhandle-sesam-test-xyzzy'
